items.briefing:
A new report has found that ASX 200 companies have been compromised by infostealer malware, which quietly steals passwords and sensitive data from infected devices. This is a reminder that even large, well-resourced Australian organisations are being targeted. Separately, a security warning has been issued about SonicWall networking equipment — even devices that have been recently patched may still be vulnerable to attackers bypassing multi-factor authentication.

Australian SMEs are also being urged not to treat cybersecurity as optional, as the cost of an incident far outweighs the cost of basic protections. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team that any SonicWall devices in use are fully updated and correctly configured.

featured.title: ASX 200 firms hit by infostealer infections: report
featured.quote: Australia's largest listed companies face credential theft and supplier chain vulnerabilities.
